tasks.label: Go out to the club with the friend group

memory.summary: The group is pushing for me to join them tonight, but my midterm tomorrow means I could really use those extra couple of hours to study. I feel torn between hanging out with friends and making sure I'm prepared.
Mina is leaving, and the club is happy I joined, so I can finally relax instead of stressing about studying tonight.
The group's relief at my joining finally lets me drop the study guilt and just relax, which feels way better than stressing out alone tonight.
Seeing the group so glad I joined makes the choice to relax tonight feel worth it, even with my midterm looming. The relief is immediate and sweet.

Seeing everyone's genuine relief at my joining makes the decision to skip studying tonight feel totally worth it, even with that midterm looming. It's a sweet, immediate comfort I haven't felt in a while.
The group's genuine relief at my joining makes skipping the study session feel like a small victory, even with that midterm looming.
Seeing everyone's relief makes skipping the midterm study session feel like a real win right now. The immediate connection is too sweet to ignore for a few hours.
Their genuine relief makes skipping the study session feel like a sweet victory right now, even with that midterm looming. This immediate connection is too good to ignore for just a few hours.
The group's relief at me skipping study feels like a sweet victory right now, even though the unfinished prep for tomorrow's midterm is still hanging over my head.

desires: Stay in the group hangout vibe tonight so I don't have to explain why I'm suddenly 'too tired' or cancel again.
Feel that warm, relieved glow from everyone accepting my choice to skip studying right now.
Not feel guilty about leaving the textbook open until tomorrow morning; the social connection is worth the risk of a bad grade.
Keep the momentum of being the 'fun one' who prioritizes us over the boring work.

goals.description: Keep the group's relief and my place in their circle intact by not being that guy who cancels plans last minute or is too stressed to hang out.
goals.rationale: The club night went well; seeing them happy validates my entire evening. If I now mention I'm tired or stressed about the exam, it risks killing the vibe they just enjoyed. My affiliation drive overrides the logical need to decompress.

goals.description: Stay up a little longer just to savor this feeling of belonging before the morning hits, even if it means trading sleep for connection.
goals.rationale: I feel safe here right now. The thought of going home to an empty room and facing the guilt of unfinished studying feels heavier than staying up late scrolling or chatting. I'll push the sleep debt again because the emotional payoff is immediate, while the cost (tiredness tomorrow) feels far away.

agent.last_reasoning: The midterm already started; I can't fix that now. Staying in the room feels heavy and isolates me from Mina and Jae who are probably hanging out. I'll just wait for them to message so I don't seem like 'that guy' ignoring the group, even if my GPA suffers.

goals.description: Slip out of the room and head to Echo immediately so I don't have to explain why I'm missing out tonight.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value is highest here; being part of the group matters more than finishing my study session. Since Jae explicitly said 'no pressure' and offered to grab me, avoiding the potential friction of a later explanation aligns with my avoidant conflict style. My impulsive nature means I'll seize this low-pressure moment now rather than waiting until tomorrow when guilt might make it harder.

goals.description: Leave the textbook open on the desk and walk away without looking back at the progress bar.
goals.rationale: I know the midterm is important (60% done), but my short planning horizon discounts the consequence of cramming for one hour tomorrow. The immediate relief of socializing outweighs the abstract risk of a lower grade right now. I won't force myself to finish the remaining 40%; that feels like work I should do 'later' anyway.

desires: Don't let the group down by missing their night out; I need to fit in with Mina and Jae.
Avoid the awkward explanation of why I'm staying home if I can just slip away later or tomorrow.
Not feel guilty about skipping this hangout, even though studying is important.
